Name: Leo
Message: Morning everyone. I'm assigned to welding station 3 today.

Name: Mack
Message: Heads up Leo, station 3 always overheats on Tuesdays after lunch.

Name: Leo
Message: Oh, I didn't see that in the manual. What should I do when it happens?

Name: Mack
Message: The official manual won't tell you. You need to drop the current by 5% to keep it stable.

Name: Sarah
Message: Actually Mack, the new SOP issued last month says if a station overheats, you should power it down completely for 15 minutes.

Name: Mack
Message: If we power it down for 15 minutes every time, we'll never hit our quota, Sarah. Dropping it 5% works fine.

Name: Leo
Message: Uh, okay. I'll just drop it 5% then?

Name: Sarah
Message: No, follow the SOP. Power it down. Safety first.

Name: Mack
Message: Man, the safety engineers who write these manuals have never actually worked a day on the floor.

Name: Leo
Message: I'm having trouble getting the control panel to register my ID card.

Name: Mack
Message: Oh that scanner is garbage. Just hit it with a heavy wrench.

Name: Leo
Message: Really? Won't that break it?

Name: Mack
Message: I'm joking, kid, don't actually hit the machine. Just wipe the sensor with a dry cloth.

Name: Leo
Message: Wiping it worked. Thanks.

Name: John
Message: Has anyone seen the blueprint for the HVAC layout?

Name: Mack
Message: Check the trailer, but honestly, I think the site dog ate it.

Name: John
Message: We have a site dog?

Name: Sarah
Message: No, John, he's venting. The blueprints are digitally loaded on your tablet.

Name: Mack
Message: I swear, if the cafeteria serves that soggy meatloaf again today, I'm quitting.

Name: Leo
Message: Wait, Mack, you're quitting?

Name: Mack
Message: Just venting, Leo. Half the stuff I say is noise.
